behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily in backoff math/state progression inside retry logic, not a required module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not inherently hide this arithmetic bug. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff calculator/retry-state function with a deterministic sequence of failures and assert delays follow 1x, 2x, 4x... and clamp at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is at a boundary between response/error parsing and retry policy decision. REAL-INFRA: no real network required if transport is stubbed. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only classifier math can miss mismatched error-shape handoff between modules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware with the real response-to-error mapping module; feed representative 429/headers payloads through the client boundary and assert it classifies as rate limit and computes expected delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires interaction of retry loop, timer/scheduler abstraction, and request executor. REAL-INFRA: can be done with fake clock and stub transport, no OS/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing resend helper alone can miss that original request context is not preserved across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Use real retry orchestration + request wrapper modules, stub transport to fail once with rate-limit then succeed, advance fake time, and assert second attempt reuses original request and `call()` resolves with success payload.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error is often produced at the boundary between retry controller and exported API error layer. REAL-INFRA: no external infra required. MOCK-HIDE: unit test of error class alone misses whether retry stack emits that type on terminal failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run retry flow with transport stubbed to keep returning rate-limit responses until max retries; assert rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` and not raw/generic upstream error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: explicit multi-module state interaction (retry counter + quota tracker + scheduler) is the failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: fake clock and bounded attempts can validate termination without real OS/network. MOCK-HIDE: isolated unit tests can miss emergent non-termination from combined state transitions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end local retry pipeline with persistent rate-limit stub, advance fake clock through all waits, and assert completion within bounded virtual time and exact expected attempt count before final error.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is fundamentally shared-state coordination across concurrent callers/modules. REAL-INFRA: no real network needed; local async concurrency suffices. MOCK-HIDE: single-function unit tests usually miss race/ownership bugs in shared bucket wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/account context, fire many concurrent `call()` invocations against a controllable stub transport, and assert aggregate permitted attempts match one shared quota (not per-caller independent quotas).

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: FIFO guarantee depends on queueing component + dispatcher interaction under async contention. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with deterministic local scheduler/fake timers. MOCK-HIDE: testing queue primitive alone can miss reorder introduced by dispatcher integration.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple calls quickly into a throttled client, control release timing with fake clock/semaphores, and assert observed dispatch order equals submission order.
